architecture_summary: Add a schema-backed ReadOnlyWorkerRuntimeEnablementSubmittedEvidenceRefs contract that consumes the status ledger, submits repo-local witness refs for review, preserves missing operator/runtime inputs, and blocks evidence acceptance, authority, runtime enablement, dispatch, worker invocation, receipt emission, receipt append, and terminal closure.
data_model_changes: Introduce submitted evidence ref records linked to source status records and source operator input ids.
Track repo-local refs submitted for review separately from missing operator evidence names.
Keep accepted/rejected evidence refs, authority grant refs, and all runtime/effect counts empty or zero.
implementation_plan: Add deterministic validator and builder derived from the status ledger projection.
Generate the Foundation Mode submitted evidence refs fixture.

Add focused tests for fixture parity, validation receipt writing, authority overclaim rejection, acceptance drift rejection, missing input overclaim rejection, missing record rejection, and CLI JSON output.
Register the schema in the protocol manifest and CI worker runtime enablement lane.
Update worker docs, schema README, gap register, and SDLC artifacts.
invariants: Submitted refs are not accepted evidence.
Foundation witness refs do not satisfy runtime enablement input names by themselves.
Dispatch, worker invocation, receipt emission, receipt append, and terminal closure remain denied.
Connector, filesystem, network, secret, and success-claim authority remain denied.
rollback_plan: Remove the submitted evidence refs schema, fixture, validator, tests, manifest entry, CI inclusion, docs updates, and SDLC artifacts.
Keep the prior status ledger artifact as the active unresolved evidence boundary.
tradeoffs: The artifact advances evidence handling from unresolved status to submitted-for-review refs without changing runtime authority.
Repository-local witness refs are useful for review but intentionally remain insufficient for runtime enablement acceptance.
